summary: Iteration 13: verified DD/MM/YYYY (slash) date format across backend + frontend. Created 6-case pytest suite (5 pass, 1 skip→pass after pdfplumber install → all 6 pass). Ran full regression across iter9/10/11/12 (66/67 pass). Verified via UI that /quotations, /orders, /quotations/{id} all render DD/MM/YYYY (0 dash occurrences). Downloaded a real quotation PDF via /api/quotations/{id}/pdf and confirmed via pdfplumber that DD/MM/YYYY dates appear in the header (Quot. Date: 23/06/2026, Valid: 31/12/2026).

minor.issue: `Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None` uses `Optional` but only `Dict, Any` are imported from typing. Currently harmless because `from __future__ import annotations` makes annotations lazy strings, but any consumer calling typing.get_type_hints() on render_quote_pdf will raise NameError. Import Optional for cleanliness.

action_items: OPTIONAL: import Optional in backend/quote_pdf.py to remove latent NameError under strict type introspection.

OPTIONAL: iter10 test_settings_integrations_exposes_test_mode is now stale (asserts RESTRICT_OUTBOUND_TO_PHONE=+918200663263 but env is intentionally blank per iter13 PR). Update or delete that assertion; unrelated to date format.
critical_code_review_comments: quote_pdf.py uses `Optional` without import (masked by `from __future__ import annotations`).
toLocaleDateString('en-GB') consistently used in Dashboard/Quotations/Orders/ContactDetail/MyQuotes — good, produces DD/MM/YYYY.
OrderView.jsx has 3 placeholder='DD/MM/YYYY' + 4 error toasts referencing DD/MM/YYYY — all consistent.

backend strftime pattern audit confirms 5/5 human-facing timestamps now use %d/%m/%Y (server.py:209, 889; dispatch.py:376, 520, 700). File-name timestamps (dispatch.py:270,298) intentionally remain %Y%m%d%H%M%S — correct.
PublicTrackingStrip.jsx retains en-IN '23 Jun 2026' format — per PR scope, intentional and correct.

success_rate.backend: 100% (6/6 new + iter13 scope) — iter10 pre-existing stale test unrelated

seed_data_creation: none — used existing quotations/orders in DB
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 13 (DD/MM/YYYY cosmetic rollout) verified end-to-end: 5 source-grep assertions, 1 helper unit test, 1 real PDF download parsed with pdfplumber. Regression run of iter9-12: 66/67 pass; the single fail (iter10 test_settings_integrations_exposes_test_mode) is stale because RESTRICT_OUTBOUND_TO_PHONE is intentionally empty now — this is documented in the review request itself. pdfplumber was pip-installed during this iteration for PDF content assertions and can be reused going forward.
rca of the issue: n/a — this was a cosmetic feature verification, no bugs found in scope.
